beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Nora starts her Monday with full energy. She's an early riser and will need time to settle in before her commute to Bellevue.

beats.activity: Drive to Bellevue clinic for morning shift.
beats.reasoning: Commute from South Seattle to Bellevue downtown; accounting for Monday morning traffic and the I-90 crossing, approximately 55 minutes.

beats.activity: Work as radiologic technologist at the clinic.
beats.reasoning: Full morning shift; Nora is focused on patient care and doesn't follow the 11am FIFA match—she's working through that time and not interested in catching it at a bar.

beats.activity: Lunch at a café in downtown Bellevue.
beats.reasoning: Nora grabs a quick lunch near the clinic; the sunny weather makes outdoor seating appealing, and she takes advantage of the break before afternoon appointments.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift work at the clinic.
beats.reasoning: Nora completes her afternoon work block; she's steady and committed to her patients and finishes the standard clinic day.
Return commute from Bellevue to South Seattle; afternoon traffic is moderate, approximately 50–55 minutes to get home.

beats.activity: Stop at neighborhood market for fresh herbs and garden supplies.
beats.reasoning: On the way home, Nora picks up supplies for her urban garden; the sunny weather has put her in the mood to tend to her plants this evening.

beats.activity: Arrive home and work in the garden.
beats.reasoning: Nora settles at home and spends the early evening tending to her urban garden in the beautiful June sunshine; it's her favorite way to decompress after a work day.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a light dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Nora cooks a simple meal using fresh ingredients from the market, staying in for a quiet Monday evening.

diary: Monday started crisp and clear—perfect coffee weather on the porch before heading out. The drive to Bellevue was smooth; I caught the tail end of someone's car radio talking about the World Cup match, which made me smile. Clinic was steady, nothing too intense. I grabbed lunch at Whole Foods, sat outside for ten minutes just breathing in the sunshine. The afternoon flew by with back-to-back appointments. On the way home, I noticed the light rail was down between Westlake and U-District, but it didn't affect me. Stopped at Safeway for basil and some new potting soil—my tomatoes are finally getting leggy, and I couldn't resist. Spent the golden hour in the garden, hands in dirt, and it felt like the day finally exhaled. Simple dinner, simple evening. The kind of Monday that doesn't ask too much.
thought_process: I was in a good headspace today—the weather helped. I didn't feel the pull to check the World Cup bars downtown even though they were probably buzzing; I'm not really a soccer person, and I knew my energy was better spent at home. The light rail closure didn't bother me since I drive. Work was routine, which I appreciated after a chaotic May. The garden has been calling to me all week, so I made sure to stop for supplies without overthinking it. By evening I was genuinely content, less about doing and more about being. My mood stayed steady and present all day—no crashes, no weird spikes. Just a Monday that fit.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
